title: Safe delete habits
summary: Learn and practice safe habits for deleting data in SQL, including previewing affected rows and using WHERE clauses to avoid accidental data loss.

sketchBlocks.title: Why Safe Delete Habits Matter
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
Deleting rows in SQL is a powerful operation—once data is deleted, it cannot be easily recovered. Accidental deletions can cause major problems, especially if you forget to use a WHERE clause and remove all rows from a table. To avoid mistakes, always preview the rows you plan to delete with a SELECT statement first. For example:

```sql
-- Preview rows marked as test data
SELECT * FROM inventory_items WHERE is_test = 1;
```

After confirming the correct rows, you can safely delete them:

```sql
DELETE FROM inventory_items WHERE is_test = 1;
```

This habit helps ensure you only remove the intended data.

sketchBlocks.title: Preview Before You Delete
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
A safe workflow for deleting data involves two steps:

1. **Preview**: Use a SELECT statement with the same WHERE condition you plan to use in your DELETE. This lets you see exactly which rows will be affected.
2. **Delete**: Only after confirming the preview, run the DELETE statement with the same WHERE clause.

Example:

```sql
SELECT name, price, id, price * id AS calculated_value
FROM inventory_items;
```

This approach reduces the risk of accidental data loss.

quizDraft.title: Safe habits when deleting data
quizDraft.prompt: Which of the following are safe habits when deleting rows in SQL? Select all that apply.
quizDraft.hint: Think about steps that help prevent accidental data loss.

help.concept: Safe delete habits include previewing affected rows, always using WHERE, and double-checking your target set.
help.hint_1: Previewing and filtering are both important.
help.hint_2: Avoid deleting without WHERE and always check which rows will be affected.

quizDraft.title: Why preview before deleting?
quizDraft.prompt: Choose the best value for the first missing blank in the statement.
quizDraft.hint: Think about what previewing helps you avoid.

help.concept: Previewing lets you see which rows will be deleted, reducing the risk of mistakes.
help.hint_1: Previewing helps you avoid unwanted deletions.
help.hint_2: It helps you the missing term accidental removal of important data.
